task_completion.description: Open the baseline propeller model in OpenVSP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP GUI is open and displays the baseline propeller model.

task_completion.description: Change the number of blades (NumBlades) from 2 to 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP geometry window shows 'NumBlades' set to 3, or the 3D viewer clearly displays a 3-blade propeller.
A save dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/exports/3blade_prop.vsp3' or the OpenVSP title bar reflecting the new filename.

task_completion.description: Open the PropBEM tool and configure the Advance Ratio (J) sweep.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PropBEM analysis window is open with the Advance Ratio (J) sweep configured: Start = 0.1, End = 1.5, and Num Pts = 15.

task_completion.description: Run the PropBEM analysis and locate the resulting CSV output.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PropBEM tool shows execution progress/completion, or a file manager/terminal displays the newly generated PropBEM CSV file.

task_completion.description: Write a summary report containing the peak efficiency and optimal J value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the creation of '/home/ga/Desktop/prop_report.txt' containing the extracted peak efficiency and corresponding Advance Ratio (J).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenVSP GUI to modify the model and run the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenVSP graphical interface, rather than just editing the .vsp3 XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened or parsed the generated CSV file to find the peak efficiency.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the CSV file opened in a spreadsheet, text editor, or being processed by a script/terminal command (e.g., cat, grep, python) to extract the values.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the report file on the Desktop.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor saving the file to the Desktop or terminal commands (like echo or nano) writing to '/home/ga/Desktop/prop_report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The target number of blades is 3. The PropBEM Advance Ratio (J) sweep must be configured with start = 0.1, end = 1.5, and exactly 15 points. The final report must contain the peak efficiency and optimal J extracted from the generated CSV.
